name: Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular through the Brugent River Protected Natural Area
name_original: Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular por el Espacio Natural Protegido del río Brugent
region_intro: Jogging around Les Planes D'Hostoles offers diverse natural landscapes within the Natural Park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone. The region features volcanic terrain, rivers, streams, and small valleys, providing varied running environments. Trails often follow old railway lines, such as the Ruta del Carrilet Greenway, which offers scenic routes through the volcanic landscape and along riverine areas. The area also includes numerous gorges and natural pools, accessible via footpaths, adding unique natural features to running routes. Best jogging routes around Les Planes D'Hostoles The most popular jogging route is Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular through the Brugent River Protected Natural Area, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the protected natural area of the Brugent River, passing by several gorges. Another top favourite among local runners is Santa Margarida Pool – Can Poetí Gorge loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This route features natural pools and gorges, offering scenic views and a varied running experience. Local runners also love the El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Les Planes D'Hostoles is defined by volcanic landscapes, riverine paths, and natural gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Les Planes D'Hostoles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Les Planes D'Hostoles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: There are over 160 running routes around Les Planes D'Hostoles, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult trails, with a few easier options as well.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Les Planes D'Hostoles are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963336612/els-gorgs-de-planes-d-hostoles-running-circular-through-the-brugent-river-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - running circular through the Brugent River Protected Natural Area&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.1-mile loop that explores the protected natural area of the Brugent River and its gorges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: The terrain is quite varied, reflecting the region's location within the Natural Park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone. You'll encounter volcanic landscapes, riverine paths, and natural gorges. Some routes follow the accessible Ruta del Carrilet Greenway, while others are more rugged, offering significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there are a few easier options. The Ruta del Carrilet Greenway, for instance, offers a relatively flat and well-maintained path, ideal for a relaxed jog or for those new to running in the area. It connects Les Planes D'Hostoles to Olot and Girona.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: Les Planes D'Hostoles is rich in natural beauty. You can explore stunning gorges and natural pools like Cogolls, Can Poeti, Plana, and Santa Margarida. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14971097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Pool – Can Poetí Gorge loop from Sant Feliu de Pallerols&lt;/a&gt; is a great example. You might also encounter volcanic landscapes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6474771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Over the Fornils Valley and Susqueda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Absolutely! The area is famous for its gorges and natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963336612/els-gorgs-de-planes-d-hostoles-running-circular-through-the-brugent-river-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles&lt;/a&gt; route specifically takes you through the protected natural area of the Brugent River, where you'll find several gorges. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24577104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swimming Area – Can Poetí Gorge loop from les Planes d'Hostoles&lt;/a&gt;, which features natural pools.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: The running routes in Les Planes D'Hostoles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the unique volcanic terrain, and the beautiful gorges and natural pools that offer stunning scenery and refreshing post-run spots.

Q: Are the running trails in Les Planes D'Hostoles suitable for families?
A: Many trails, especially sections of the Ruta del Carrilet Greenway, are suitable for families due to their relatively flat and well-maintained surfaces. However, some routes, particularly those leading to gorges or through mountainous terrain, can be moderate to difficult and might require good fitness and sure-footedness, so it's best to check individual route details.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes?
A: While specific parking information for every route isn't always listed, Les Planes D'Hostoles is generally well-equipped for visitors. Many trailheads, especially those for popular routes like the Ruta del Carrilet or access points to the gorges, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking details.

Q: Can I reach the running routes using public transport?
A: Les Planes D'Hostoles has public transport connections, which can help you access the region. The Ruta del Carrilet Greenway, for example, connects the town to larger hubs like Olot and Girona, which are accessible by bus. For specific trailheads, you might need to combine public transport with a short walk or local taxi service.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: Les Planes D'Hostoles is an excellent destination for outdoor activities year-round. However, for running, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. During peak season (June to September), natural pools and gorges can be very popular, and some protected areas may require reservations. Winter running is also possible, but some higher elevation trails might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962108907/el-far-loop-from-sant-marti-sacalm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Far Loop from Sant Martí Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a 6.9-mile trail leading through mountainous terrain with considerable ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14975237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Anna – Sant Martí Sacalm loop from Sant Martí Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 500 meters of elevation gain.
tips: This beautiful natural water pool formed by two waterfalls is the most easily accessible of the pools in the Plains of Hostoles. It is an ideal route to go with the whole family.

This waterfall is just five minutes from the Gorg de la Plana. If you visit the first, you have to visit the second, which is a little more spectacular. You need a reservation to visit. More information here: espaiprotegitdelbrugent.cat/es.
One of the natural pools along the Brugent River. It is located in the municipality of Plains d'Hostoles

The waters of the Cogolls stream are filtered and become groundwater in the area. They are enriched with dissolved calcium bicarbonate, which petrifies the plants and shapes the travertines of the waterfall. It is one of the most beautiful pools in the Planes d'Hostoles.

Natural pool located in the municipality of Planes d'Hostoles. It is in the course of the Brugent River and when it carries enough water, a waterfall of considerable dimensions is formed that makes this place a spectacular site.
Its access is from the El Carrilet Greenway from Girona to Olot.

waypoints.name: La Laura Pool and the Gran de Can Paulí Weir
